name: Motor
what_it_does: Async Python driver for MongoDB — provides non-blocking MongoDB access using asyncio (and Tornado). Motor wraps PyMongo with coroutine-based async API: `await collection.insert_one(doc)`, `async for doc in collection.find({'status': 'active'})`, `await collection.find_one({'_id': id})`. Motor is the official async MongoDB driver from MongoDB Inc. Works with FastAPI, aiohttp, Starlette, and any asyncio-based Python framework. AsyncIOMotorClient for asyncio (most common); TornadoMotorClient for Tornado. Same API semantics as PyMongo but all I/O operations are async. Required for building non-blocking agent services with MongoDB backend.
use_cases: FastAPI agent service with async MongoDB — AsyncIOMotorClient for FastAPI agent endpoints; await db.agents.find_one({'id': agent_id}) in async def route handler; non-blocking MongoDB access prevents FastAPI event loop blocking for agent API

Async agent document streaming — async for doc in db.agents.find({'active': True}): process(doc) streams large agent collections without loading all into memory; motor async cursor handles backpressure for agent batch processing

Agent session storage in MongoDB — await db.sessions.insert_one({'agent_id': id, 'context': context, 'created_at': datetime.utcnow()}) stores agent conversation state asynchronously in MongoDB without blocking event loop

Concurrent agent MongoDB operations — asyncio.gather(db.agents.find_one(id1), db.agents.find_one(id2)) runs parallel MongoDB queries for multiple agents simultaneously; Motor async I/O enables agent data loading concurrency

Motor with Beanie ODM — AsyncIOMotorClient as Motor client for Beanie document models; Agent.find(Agent.active == True).to_list() combines Motor's async driver with Beanie's type-safe document interface for agent service
not_for: Synchronous Python applications — Motor is async-only; for sync Flask or Django (non-async) agent apps, use PyMongo directly; Motor async functions cannot be called from sync context without event loop
Relational data with joins — MongoDB/Motor is document-oriented; for agent data requiring complex joins, transactions across multiple collections, or relational integrity, use PostgreSQL with asyncpg
Simple scripts — Motor requires running event loop; for one-off agent data scripts without async infrastructure, PyMongo is simpler
best_when: You're building async Python agent services (FastAPI, aiohttp) with MongoDB — Motor provides non-blocking MongoDB access that keeps async event loops responsive under concurrent agent load.
avoid_when: Your Python framework is synchronous (Flask, Django without async views), you're writing scripts without event loops, or your data model is relational.

auth.notes: Motor auth via MongoDB connection string: mongodb+srv://user:password@cluster.mongodb.net or AsyncIOMotorClient(host, username=, password=, authSource=). Atlas uses connection string with credentials.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Completely free, Apache 2.0 licensed. MongoDB Atlas hosting has separate pricing.
pricing.notes: Motor is Apache 2.0 licensed, maintained by MongoDB Inc. Free for all use.

performance.rate_limits: No library rate limits. Motor uses connection pool (default 100 connections); configure maxPoolSize for high-concurrency agent services. MongoDB Atlas has rate limits based on cluster tier.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Motor raises pymongo exceptions asynchronously: OperationFailure for MongoDB errors, ConnectionFailure for network issues, WriteError for insert/update failures. DuplicateKeyError for unique constraint violations. ServerSelectionTimeoutError when MongoDB is unreachable with timeout and hosts tried.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: find/find_one are idempotent. insert_one with custom _id is idempotent if DuplicateKeyError is caught. update_one with upsert=True provides idempotent upsert. replace_one is idempotent for same document. Design agent operations with idempotency using unique _id fields.
known_agent_gotchas: AsyncIOMotorClient must be created once and shared — creating AsyncIOMotorClient per request creates separate connection pools exhausting MongoDB connections; create client at application startup as singleton and share via FastAPI dependency injection for all agent route handlers

Async cursor must be explicitly closed or used as async context manager — async for doc in collection.find() automatically closes cursor; manual cursor operations require await cursor.close() or async with collection.find() as cursor; unclosed motor cursors cause resource leaks in long-running agent services

Motor does not support asyncio.run() with same event loop — AsyncIOMotorClient is bound to event loop it's created on; running Motor operations in different event loop (e.g., asyncio.run() in tests) from client creation raises RuntimeError; use pytest-asyncio or create new client per test for agent service testing

Bulk operations require motor.motor_asyncio not pymongo — PyMongo InsertMany cannot be awaited; must use AsyncIOMotorCollection.insert_many() not collection.bulk_write() with PyMongo operations; agent bulk data import must use Motor's async bulk API exclusively

Change streams require explicit resume token handling — db.agents.watch() returns async change stream iterator; network interruption causes StopAsyncIteration without resume token; persist resume_token and pass to watch(resume_after=token) to resume agent change stream monitoring without missing events

Connection string credentials must be URL-encoded — agent service MongoDB passwords with special characters (@, /, :, ?) must be urllib.parse.quote_plus encoded in connection string; unencoded special chars cause Motor to misparse connection string and fail to connect with cryptic URI parse error

security_score_components.security_notes: Never hardcode MongoDB credentials in agent code — use environment variables for connection string. Motor supports TLS/SSL (tls=True in connection string) — always use TLS for agent production MongoDB connections. MongoDB Atlas enforces TLS by default. Enable MongoDB authentication and create least-privilege roles for agent service database users.
